title: Dry-slick core model with separate wet/testing fallback
summary: FIA rules make dry-race pit logic structurally different because two slick compounds are mandatory, while wet races remove that constraint.
rationale: FIA rules make dry-race pit logic structurally different because two slick compounds are mandatory, while wet races remove that constraint. Recent pit-stop modeling work explicitly dropped wet/intermediate laps as reactive noise, and pre-season testing articles note teams may run unrealistic compound programs.
implementation_hint: Train the main model on slick race laps only, or heavily upweight them. Route `INTERMEDIATE`/`WET` rows and `Race == Pre-Season Testing` through a small fallback expert or simple heuristic layer; include explicit domain flags if full exclusion is too aggressive.
expected_effect: Should sharpen the main decision boundary by removing small, heterogeneous regimes that contaminate dry-race ranking.
risk: Fallback subsets are small, so their estimates may be unstable; if the test set contains many wet/testing rows, over-pruning could hurt.
